title: cordylanthus tenuis subsp. brunneus
intro: Cordylanthus tenuis subsp. brunneus, also known as the Bruneau manzanita, is a plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Cordylanthus tenuis subsp. brunneus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for herbal use by drying the leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
